methodology: Platform-product OKRs with a small set of company objectives, outcome-oriented key results, and explicit team ownership across platform product areas.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe platform outcomes for internal customers, not internal feature checklists.
Key results focus on adoption, flow, controls, and reliability rather than tool rollout volume.
Every key result assigns executing and supporting teams so shared ownership stays concrete.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make the platform the easiest path for mainstream teams
companyObjectives.statement: Improve onboarding, discoverability, and paved-road quality so stream-aligned teams choose the platform because it reduces real work.

inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows that platform adoption depends on product-quality onboarding and visible reduction of setup work.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make the standard path safer and more operable by default
companyObjectives.statement: Increase platform-wide control coverage, telemetry consistency, and incident response quality without pushing teams back into manual governance queues.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Trusted supply chain baseline: validated discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows teams accept stronger controls when those controls are built into the default path.
Shapes the exception model required to keep controls usable.

objectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make team onboarding self-service by default
objectives.objective: Reduce the bootstrap work required for mainstream product teams so onboarding to the platform becomes the fast path rather than a side project.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Measures how quickly teams become productive on the platform.
Share of onboarding and setup flows completed without platform intervention.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Share of mainstream product teams using the standard platform path.
Number of platform-help interventions required to complete team onboarding.

keyResults.target: Shorten team bootstrap from kickoff to first useful service running on the paved road
keyResults.baseline: Teams still spend too much time assembling ownership, templates, and setup context across multiple tools.
keyResults.statement: Reduce onboarding time by consolidating portal entry points, templates, and ownership defaults into one clear self-service path.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce onboarding time through clearer entry points, metadata, and self-service flow design.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by improving template quality and reducing the need for bespoke setup decisions.
Support the KR with field evidence on onboarding failure points and migration friction.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is central to Q1 platform adoption and should behave like a delivery commitment with explicit follow-through.

keyResults.target: Raise the share of teams completing core setup without platform intervention
keyResults.baseline: Too many teams still escalate routine onboarding and setup steps into ticket-based help.
keyResults.statement: Increase self-service completion by making templates, workflows, and diagnostics usable enough for mainstream teams to finish setup on their own.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to make the standard setup flow complete and usable for the most common service types.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR with portal workflow quality, service discovery, and guidance improvements.
Support the KR with telemetry on setup abandonment and workflow bottlenecks.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should improve through product quality, iteration, and evidence rather than deadline-only delivery pressure.

keyResults.target: Increase the number of mainstream teams choosing the standard platform path
keyResults.baseline: Adoption grows unevenly when onboarding quality and setup confidence vary by team archetype.
keyResults.statement: Increase platform adoption by making onboarding fast enough and support-light enough that platform use is clearly cheaper than local setup.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to make the front door to the platform credible and adoption-oriented.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by reducing variance in the standard service path.
Support the KR through migration coaching and field feedback loops that sharpen the paved road.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR reflects a product-outcome signal and should compound as onboarding quality improves.

inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Explains where teams still treat onboarding as a project instead of a self-service workflow.

objectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make runtime delivery self-service credible for mainstream services
objectives.objective: Reduce delivery and environment waiting time so staff engineers and tech leads can ship through the standard platform path without maintaining bespoke runtime logic.

linkedCustomerKPIs.kpiName: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Elapsed time from a new service starting to its first production release on the platform.
Engineering effort spent keeping custom delivery pipelines working outside the platform standard path.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Time required for a code change to reach production through the platform path.
Waiting time for standard environment creation and readiness.

keyResults.metric: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
keyResults.target: Shorten the path from service start to first production release on the standard runtime
keyResults.baseline: Teams still lose time stitching together environments, delivery rules, and dependency access.
keyResults.statement: Reduce time to first production deployment by packaging environment setup, deployment automation, and connectivity defaults into a more complete paved road.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to shorten startup-to-production time through faster environment creation and fewer setup gaps.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by making release automation and rollout evidence more reusable.
Support the KR by reducing dependency and networking setup friction.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR directly determines whether mainstream teams see the platform runtime path as viable for real delivery work.

keyResults.target: Reduce the need for local pipeline ownership outside the platform standard
keyResults.baseline: Several teams still keep custom delivery logic alive because the platform path feels incomplete for their service shape.
keyResults.statement: Decrease bespoke maintenance effort by expanding the standard release path to cover more real-world service cases without local scripting and exception handling.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce custom pipeline upkeep by turning repeated exceptions into reusable platform defaults.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by removing environment-related exceptions that push teams into local workarounds.
Support the KR by simplifying approved dependency and network patterns.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should improve through iterative reduction of platform escape hatches rather than one fixed deadline event.

keyResults.target: Lower the waiting time added by platform delivery and environment workflows
keyResults.baseline: Lead time still stretches when release and environment primitives are incomplete or hard to trust.
keyResults.statement: Reduce lead time for change by making the standard runtime path more complete, safer by default, and less dependent on manual approval or repair work.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to lower end-to-end change lead time through more standard release automation.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by cutting environment wait states and failure modes.
Support the KR by turning common policy controls into defaults instead of manual gates.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should move as the platform reduces waiting, retries, and exception paths.

inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows where the runtime path still falls back to manual coordination and review.
